item.title: 社交互动的必要性 | The need for social interaction
item.description:
这是一篇关于社交互动需求的固执己见的文章。

前几天我收到 Sandro 的消息，告诉我他在荷兰，问我们是否可以见面喝杯咖啡。

我总是想喝杯咖啡，所以我们来吧。

我从我的城镇乘火车到阿姆斯特丹，大约需要 2.5 小时。 在这段时间里，你看到了很多人。

他们中的大多数人都在他们的小泡泡里，他们的思想在不同的世界里，听着播客、音乐或看连续剧。

如果您决定全裸上车，您甚至会认为他们不会注意到这一点。

抵达阿姆斯特丹。 你很快就会注意到同样的行为。 每个人都带着 AirPods、头戴式耳机四处走动，只是在外出。

有趣的是，您甚至可以看到它与一群人一起来到阿姆斯特丹享受这座城市。

相反

考虑到这种孤独状态，我更进一步，研究了一些整体趋势。

  • 希望举办咖啡聊天以结识他人的个人
  • 想亲自见面的人
  • 科技活动开始受到更多关注
  • 现场 youtube/twitch/Instagram 互动

仔细想想，这是有道理的。
我们大多数人都处于封锁状态，不再在办公室，也不再与朋友见面。

所以我们渴望一些社交互动来平衡我们的孤独。

但这会导致复杂的行为，我们一方面想要建立真正的联系，另一方面，我们不想被外界打扰。

那么我们需要什么？

我认为我们都需要以某种方式进行社交互动。
我们想和懂的人谈谈我们在现实世界中的专业意见。

这可能有多种原因，其中一些可能是：

  • 知识验证
  • 话题讨论
  • 向他人学习

这是完全有道理的。 这些是你通常可能从办公室得到的东西。

甚至在工作开始之前，您都会快速与某人聊天。 这变得有点困难了。
您不会计划在线会议来与某人讨论与项目无关的事情。

所以这就是为什么我认为这些基本的世界互动很有意义。
这是提出问题、与他人互动以及向人们表达您的想法的绝佳方式。

我们应该！

这是改善、成长和建立真正联系的好方法。

因此选择更多的互动！
但不要将自己局限于自己的专业领域。 也许试着和火车上那个随机的陌生人交谈！

谁知道你会遇到什么样的有趣的人。

结论

我们似乎对自己变得更加独立，与周围的世界隔离开来，但渴望实时参与来验证和与拥有相同信念的人进行讨论。

考虑到我们在某种程度上孤独地度过了两年，您对当今时代的社交互动有何看法？

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

This is an opinionated article on the need for social interaction.

The other day I got a message from Sandro, telling me he's in The Netherlands and asking if we could meet for a coffee.

I'm always up for a coffee, so let's do it.

I took the train from my town to Amsterdam, which is roughly 2.5 hours of travel. During this time you see so many people.

And most of them are in their little bubble, their minds in different worlds, listening to a podcast, music, or watching a series.

You'd even think they wouldn't notice it if you decide to enter the train fully naked.

(I didn't try that)

Arrive in Amsterdam. You quickly notice the same behavior. Everyone is walking around with AirPods, overhead earphones, and just zoning out.

The interesting part is that you even see it with groups of people that came to Amsterdam together to enjoy the city.

The opposite

Considering this state of solitude, I took it one step further and looked at some overall trends.

  • Twitter Spaces are becoming more and more popular.
  • Individuals wanting to host coffee chats to meet others
  • People wanting to meet up in person
  • Tech events are starting to catch more traction
  • Live youtube/twitch/Instagram interactions

And thinking about it, it makes sense.
Most of us have been in lockdowns, no longer in the office, and no longer meeting up with friends.

So we crave some social interaction to counterbalance our solitude.

But this makes for complicated behavior, one side of us wants to make that genuine connection, but on the other side, we don't want to get bothered by the outside world.

So what do we need?

I think we all need that social interaction in some way.
We want to talk about our professional opinions in the real world with people who understand.

This can have multiple reasons of which some could be:

  • validation of knowledge
  • discussion on topics
  • learning from others

And it makes total sense. These were things you would generally perhaps get from an office.

You would quickly chat to someone before work even started. This has become a bit more difficult.
You're not going to plan an online meeting to discuss non-project-related things with someone.

So that's why I think these fundamental world interactions make a lot of sense.
It's an excellent way to ask questions, interact with others, and spew your ideas at people.

And we should!

It's a great way to improve, grow and make a genuine connection.

Thus opt for some more interaction!
But don't limit yourself to just your field of expertise. Perhaps try and talk to that random stranger on the train!

Who knows what kind of interesting people you meet like that.

Conclusion

We seem to have become more to ourselves, zoning out from the world around us, yet crave real-time engagement to verify and discuss with people that share the same beliefs.

How do you feel about social interaction in this day and age, considering we spend two years in somewhat solitude?

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter
item.author: dailydevtips1

item.title: 我一直在 React.js 中以错误的方式创建表单🤔 | I was creating Forms the wrong way all along in React.js 🤔
item.description:
当我创建一个注册表单时，我发现自己创建了几十个 useStates，然后为这些 useStates 创建了几十个 onChange 处理程序。 像这样的🤮

伙计，即使写这个是为了说明，我也感到困倦！

另外，不客气，你用那个可怕的One Light主题代码片段大开眼界。 一些白色是有利于注意的！ 所以......你明白了，在这篇文章中，我将尝试以一种优雅的方式解决这个问题（当然不是我在上一篇文章中所做的 BS，制作一个有问题的 React Form 组件，甚至没有人费心去看一下！）

让我们开始吧！

代码

export default function App () { // NOT a even a SINGLE useState babyyyyyyy! 😎 const submitForm = ( e ) => { e . preventDefault (); const formData = new FormData ( e . target ); const inputObject = Object . fromEntries ( formData ); // convert the FormData object to a JSON object console . log ( inputObject ); }; return ( < div className = " App " > < form onSubmit = { submitForm } > < div > < input name = " email " placeholder = " email " /> < /div > < div > < input name = " password " placeholder = " password " /> < /div > < div > < input name = " phone " placeholder = " phone " /> < /div > < br /> < button type = " submit " > Submit < /button > < /form > < /div > ); }

您可以在查看这篇文章的同一浏览器中尝试此代码并对其进行修改，这要感谢代码和框（代码链接）

对于刚接触 React 的初学者朋友，我们在这里所做的是：

  • 将输入字段包装在实际的 HTML form元素中
  • 定义每个输入字段的name属性（可以是任何东西，HTML 使用此属性来命名输入值）
  • 创建一个没有onClick处理程序但type属性设置为'submit'的按钮
  • 在form元素下定义一个onSubmit处理程序

在用户输入完他们的详细信息后，单击表单中声明的type='submit'按钮，将导致 HTML form元素调用其onSubmit处理程序，即我们的submitForm函数。

 const submitForm = ( e ) => { // 1 e . preventDefault (); // 2 const formData = new FormData ( e . target ); // 3 const inputObject = Object . fromEntries ( formData ); // convert the FormData object to a JSON object console . log ( inputObject ); };

现在，我们在这里做了 3 件事：

  1. 调用 HTML FormEvent类型的preventDefault方法，由 HTML Goddess 自己作为参数传递给我们的函数（我们将其命名为e ）。 此功能可防止表单在提交后继续其默认行为，其中包括使用表单输入值作为有效负载自动向同一页面的 URL 发出 GET 请求并重新加载页面（我们不希望这样，因为我们正在定义自己的表单提交逻辑，其功能将被页面重新加载中断）

  2. 在FormData构造函数中传递表单元素（引用为e.target ）并将其存储在我们的formData常量中。
    这将获取输入元素并将它们解析为键值对，其中键是我们在name属性中针对我们的输入定义的值，值将是它们对应的输入文本。 所有不同的输入文本值都可以使用它们的名称来检索，如下所示：

 // quite similar to the map syntax to get a key ( map.get("key") ) const email = formData . get ( " email " )

这不是很神奇和优雅吗？ 记录现在创建的对象，给出以下输出：

有用！

但！ 这是一个很大的但（双关语），这种方法的缺点是你不能写Controlled Inputs 。 为此，您必须声明一个useState & 它对应的onChange处理程序。

看看这个例子

这是 React js 中的受控输入，但对于这样的输入，我们可以使用混合方法：

  • 定义表单中的所有输入
  • 仅为那些受控制的输入编写useState和onChange处理程序
  • 然后，手动将 formData 中的值设置为那些受控状态变量

看到 HTML 自动为我们处理输入似乎是一种魔法，老实说，我讨厌魔法发生但为了让开发人员体验良好，一些魔法是可以忍受的👌🏻

喜欢这篇文章，如果你喜欢它🙃

但如果你喜欢它呢？ 伙计，你必须在Twitter 上关注我😉



原文:

When I was creating a signup form, I found myself creating dozens of useStates & then creating dozens of onChange handlers for those useStates. Something like this 🤮

Man I feel sleepy even writing this for illustration!

Also, you're welcome, for blasting your eyes with that monstrous One Light theme code snippet. Some amount of white is good for attention aye! 😉

So... you get the point, In this post, I'll be trying to solve this problem in an elegant manner (certainly not the BS I did in my previous post, making a buggy React Form component which no one even bothers to have a look at!)

Let's Get Started!

Code 

export default function App() { // NOT a even a SINGLE useState babyyyyyyy! 😎 const submitForm = (e) => { e.preventDefault(); const formData = new FormData(e.target); const inputObject = Object.fromEntries(formData); // convert the FormData object to a JSON object console.log(inputObject); }; return ( <div className="App"> <form onSubmit={submitForm}> <div> <input name="email" placeholder="email" /> </div>
 <div> <input name="password" placeholder="password" /> </div>
 <div> <input name="phone" placeholder="phone" /> </div>
 <br /> <button type="submit">Submit</button>
 </form>
 </div>
 );
}

You can try this code & tinker with it in the same browser as you're viewing this post, thanks to codesandbox (code link)

For my beginner friends, who are new to React, what we did here was:

  • wrap the input fields in an actual HTML form element
  • define the name attribute of each of the input fields (can be anything, HTML uses this attribute to name the input value against it)
  • create a button with no onClick handler but a type attribute set to 'submit'
  • define an onSubmit handler under the form element

After the user has done typing their details in the input, clicking on the button with type='submit' declared in the form, will cause the HTML form element to call its onSubmit handler i.e our submitForm function.

const submitForm = (e) => { // 1 e.preventDefault(); // 2 const formData = new FormData(e.target); // 3 const inputObject = Object.fromEntries(formData); // convert the FormData object to a JSON object console.log(inputObject);
};

Now, we've done 3 things here:

  1. call the preventDefault method of the HTML FormEvent type, passed as an argument into our function by the HTML Goddess herself (we named it e). This function prevents the form from continuing its default behaviour after submission which includes automatically making a GET request to the same page's URL with the form input values as payload AND reloading the page (we don't want that because we're defining our own form submit logic whose functioning will be interrupted by a page reload)

  2. Pass the form element (referenced as e.target) in a FormData constructor & store it in our formData constant.
    This will take the input elements and parse them into key-value pairs where the key is what we defined in the name attribute against our inputs & the value will be their corresponding input text. All the different input text values can be retrieved using their name, something like this:

// quite similar to the map syntax to get a key ( map.get("key") )
const email = formData.get("email")

Isn't that quite magical AND elegant? HTML does all the work for you from parsing the form input values to collecting the data & returning it in a map-like structure 🪄.=

3.Last but not least, we convert the FormData object which has our input values, to a plain JavaScript object with Object.fromEntries( ... ). Logging the, now created object, gives this output:

IT WORKS!

BUT! and that's a big but (pun intended), the cons of this approach is that you CAN'T write Controlled Inputs. For that, you HAVE TO declare a useState & It's corresponding onChange handler.

"What the hell is a controlled Input?"

Have a look at this example

This is a controlled input in React js, but for inputs like this, we can use the hybrid approach:

  • Define all the inputs in a form
  • Write useState & onChange handler ONLY for those inputs that are controlled
  • Then, manually set values in the formData to those controlled state variables

P.S:
Seeing HTML automatically handling inputs for us appears to be some kind of magic and to be honest, I hate when magic is occurring BUT some amount of magic is bearable for the sake of making the developer experience good 👌🏻

Aaaand... that's a wrap! Like this post, if you liked it 🙃

But if you loved it? man you gotta follow me on Twitter 😉
item.author: kuvambhardwaj

item.title: GitLab 将删除休眠项目 | GitLab will be deleting dormant projects
item.description:
一旦暂停，本将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，本将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，本的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ben 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 Ben 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ben 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Once suspended, ben will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ben will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ben will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ben is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

Thanks for keeping DEV Community safe. Here is what you can do to flag ben:

ben consistently posts content that violates DEV Community's code of conduct because it is harassing, offensive or spammy.

Report other inappropriate conduct

Unflagging ben will restore default visibility to their posts.

item.title: 使用产品和价格为您的 SaaS 业务建模 | Modeling your SaaS business with Products and Prices
item.description:
正如我们在入门中了解到的，产品和价格是许多 Stripe 集成的核心资源。 它们与结帐会话、发票、支付链接、订阅和新的订单 API 密切相关。

现在，您将学习如何创建和管理产品和价格，以便能够利用客户门户、嵌入式定价表、追加销售等。

最后，我将分享一个捷径，用于使用Stripe CLI 固定装置搭建一个基于良好价值的 SaaS 商业模型，敬请期待（或直接滚动到底部）。

处理产品和价格
您可以在仪表板中或通过 API 创建和更新产品和价格。 您实际上只需要在设置新集成或推出新产品时创建新产品和价格。 在本文中，我们将使用 Stripe CLI 进行 API 调用。

  • 一些高级用例，例如创建特别价格，需要您使用 API。 如果您有大量产品和价格，或者您正在构建与 Elements 的自定义集成，则需要使用 API。
  • 如果您想避免编写代码或只有少数产品和价格，请使用仪表板创建和管理产品和价格。 在测试模式下设置您的定价模型，然后单击产品详细信息页面上的“复制到实时模式”按钮。

什么是产品？
产品描述了您向客户提供的特定商品或服务。 我倾向于使用术语“级别”、“层级”和“计划”来代表客户正在购买的产品。 如果您正在处理更旧的 Stripe 集成，您可能还会看到一个 Plan 对象。 产品需要名称，并且可以选择具有其他几个属性，例如描述、图像等。 大多数 SaaS 定价表显示给定订阅级别包含的功能列表。 您可以指定与产品相关的功能列表。

如果您是 SaaS 平台，您可能有基本层和高级层。 如果基本和高级都提供独特的属性或功能，它们将被建模为单独的产品。 您可能还需要设置费或一次性培训服务。 一次性费用也配置为产品——我们将在下一篇文章中介绍一次性费用。

产品也可以有关联的税码。 有几个与 SaaS 相关，它们与 Stripe Tax 一起使用以得出要应用的正确税款。
每个产品必须有一个（或多个）相关的价格，并且价格代表的远远超过金额。

什么是价格？
当您想到“价格”时，您可能会想到一个数字，即某物的成本。 在 Stripe 中，价格不仅仅是一个数量。 价格封装了客户支付的金额和频率（使用给定的货币）。

价格可以有一个固定的unit_amount或可以使用等级。 在这种情况下，层级与更好更好的 SaaS 层级无关，而是一个单独的分级定价模型，其中第一个 X 的成本是固定的，然后 Y 的成本会少一点，Z 的成本会更低。如果你很好奇关于分级定价或基于数量的定价的分层定价，请查看Stripe 文档以获取更多信息。

我们将使用固定unit_amount模拟我们的 SaaS 定价。 订阅价格是重复的，您必须指定一个时间间隔，例如month或year ，以了解向客户收费的频率。

如果使用 Stripe Tax，您还需要为价格指定tax_behavior以确定税款是否已包含在金额中（ inclusive ），或者是否应添加税款（ exclusive ）。

为什么正确建模定价很重要
例如，客户门户设置期望每个定价级别都有一个单独的产品，在给定的重复间隔内有自己的价格。

此外，如果您想直接在 Checkout 中启用从月度到年度的追加销售，则必须以特定方式对定价进行建模。

最后，正确建立业务模型可确保它与新的可嵌入定价表很好地配合。

为 SaaS 应用程序建模定价
大多数软件即服务平台根据为客户创造的价值提供不同的定价。 想象一下，我们正在开始一项新的基于订阅的业务。

首先，我们将专注于保持商业模式非常简单，并且只提供单一价格的月度订阅。 我们需要可以使用 Stripe CLI 创建的 1 个产品和 1 个价格：

 stripe products create --name "Startup" --description "The essentials for when you're just getting started."

该命令的输出是新创建的产品的 JSON，其中将包含一个类似于“prod_abc123”的 ID。 我们将使用该 ID 创建相关的价格：

 stripe prices create --product prod_abc123 --unit-amount 500 -d "recurring[interval]=month" --currency USD --tax-behavior=exclusive

这是存储我们稍后将用于设置订阅的产品和价格的最简单方法。 从月度计划开始很好，但我们知道，通过还提供年度计划，我们将获得更好的可预测性、更多的现金流和更低的会计成本，因为我们只需要每年而不是每月进行一次对账。 我们提供的服务将完全相同，客户现在只有两种选择，每月支付一次或每年支付一次。 我们可以通过提供比每月订阅小幅折扣来说服客户选择年度定价。

现在我们面临一个决定：为现有产品创建一个新价格？ 或者为年度计划创建新产品和新价格？

答案是：为现有产品创建一个新价格。 由于包年套餐提供的功能集和价值与包月套餐相同，我们应该创建一个新价格。

 stripe prices create --product prod_KcyHvtjwKmIAQx --unit-amount 5000 -d "recurring[interval]=year" --currency USD --tax-behavior=exclusive

有了这个单一的产品，我们现在有两个价格点，客户可以订阅按月或按年付费。

这释放了正确建模的第一个好处：追加销售。 由于我们想鼓励用户购买包年套餐，我们可以更新包月价格并将其设置为加售至包年价格。

注意：API 不支持配置追加销售，仅用于仪表板。 如果您想使用 API 进行追加销售，我们很想了解有关您的用例的更多信息，因此请联系： @cjav_dev ！

这是客户在订阅时看到的内容：

随着产品的改进并在更成熟的客户中获得吸引力，我们需要重新调整产品的价值与收取的价格。 一种方法是提高所有新客户的价格。 我们将在以后的文章中讨论如何使用查找键来试验价格变化。 目前，我们想开始为不同细分市场的客户提供差异化​​服务。 我们将创建一个新的企业层，其中包括专门的支持，这是一个非常适合大客户需求的功能集。

由于此新产品将为客户提供不同的价值，因此我们希望创建具有自己价格的新产品。 我们还希望为企业客户提供按月或按年付费的选项。 让我们创建一个新产品及其月度和年度价格。

 stripe products create --name "Business" --description "For businesses looking to achieve maximum efficiency and time savings."
 stripe prices create --product prod_abcdef --unit-amount 3200 -d "recurring[interval]=month" --currency USD --tax-behavior=exclusive
 stripe prices create --product prod_abcdef --unit-amount 32000 -d "recurring[interval]=year" --currency USD --tax-behavior=exclusive

最后一个考虑因素是货币。 如果我们想为客户提供与为现有价格配置的货币不同的货币支付的能力，那么我们需要为现有产品创建新价格。 例如，我们可能希望为我们的月度和年度计划创建一个 28 欧元的价格和一个 280 欧元的价格，以便我们的欧洲客户可以用欧元而不是美元支付。
您可以在第三层、第四层或第五层遵循相同的模式。

您还可以将此固件文件与 Stripe CLI 一起使用，以快速构建一些示例产品和价格：

 stripe fixtures value-based-saas-pricing-seed.json

在下一篇文章中，我们将讨论一些常见问题，例如如何对设置费用或中期计费周期一次性费用进行建模。

关于作者

他喜欢学习和教授新的编程语言和 Web 框架。 当他不在电脑前时，他会与家人共度时光或骑自行车🚲。



原文:

As we learned in the primer, Products and Prices are core resources for many Stripe integrations. They’re closely associated with Checkout Sessions, Invoices, Payment Links, Subscriptions, and the new Orders API.

Now, you’ll learn how to create and manage Products and Prices so that you’ll be able to take advantage of the customer portal, embeddable pricing tables, up-sells, and more.

At the end I’ll share a shortcut for scaffolding a good-better-best value based SaaS business model using a Stripe CLI fixture, so stay tuned (or just scroll to the bottom).

Working with products and prices
You can create and update products and prices in the Dashboard or through the API. You really only need to create new Products and Prices when setting up a new integration or if you launch a new product offering. In this article, we’ll use the Stripe CLI to make API calls.

  • Some advanced use cases, like creating ad-hoc prices, require you to use the API. If you have a large number of products and prices or if you’re building a custom integration with Elements, you need to use the API.
  • Use the Dashboard to create and manage products and prices, if you want to avoid writing code or if you only have a few products and prices. Set up your pricing model in test mode and click the “Copy to live mode” button on the product details page.

What is a Product?
Products describe the specific goods or services you offer to your customers. I tend to use the terms “level”, “tier”, and “plan” all to represent the Product that a customer is purchasing. If you’re working on a much older Stripe integration, you may also see a Plan object. Plan is a legacy object that is replaced with the newer and more flexible Price object, so you should avoid using Plans and instead use Prices.

A Product requires a name, and can optionally have several other attributes like description, images, and more. Most SaaS pricing tables display a list of features that are included for a given subscription level. You can specify a list of features related to a Product.

If you’re a SaaS platform you might have basic and premium tiers. Both basic and premium would be modeled as separate products if they offer unique attributes or features. You may also require a set-up fee or one-time training service. One-time fees are also configured as Products — we’ll cover one-time fees in the next article.

Products can also have an associated tax-code. There are several related to SaaS and they are used with Stripe Tax to derive the correct taxes to apply.
Each Product must have one (or more) related Prices, and a Price represents much more than the amount.

What is a Price?
When you think of “price” you probably think of a number for how much something costs. In Stripe, a Price is a little more than just an amount. Prices encapsulate how much and how often the customer will pay (with a given currency).

Prices can have a fixed unit_amount or can use tiers. Tiers in this case are not related to good-better-best SaaS tiers, but a separate graduated pricing model where the cost is fixed for the first X, then a little less for Y, and even less for Z. If you’re curious about tiered pricing for graduated or volume based pricing, please check out the Stripe documentation for more information.

We’ll model our SaaS pricing with fixed unit_amount. Prices for Subscriptions are recurring and you must specify an interval like month or year for how frequently the customer will be charged.

If using Stripe Tax, you’ll also want to specify the tax_behavior for the Price to determine whether the tax is already included in the amount (inclusive), or if tax should be added (exclusive).

Why modeling your pricing correctly matters
Certain features of Stripe expect your pricing to be modeled in a specific way. For instance, customer portal settings expect that each pricing level has a separate product with its own prices at their given recurring intervals.

Also, if you want to enable upsells from monthly to annual, directly in Checkout, pricing must be modeled in a specific way.

Lastly, getting the modeling of your business right ensures it’ll play nicely with the new embeddable pricing table.

Modeling pricing for a SaaS application
Most software-as-a-service platforms offer different pricing depending on the value created for the customer. Imagine we’re starting a new subscription-based business.

At first we’ll focus on keeping the business model very simple and only offer monthly subscriptions for a single price. We’ll need 1 product and 1 price which we can create with the Stripe CLI:

stripe products create --name "Startup" --description "The essentials for when you're just getting started."

The output of that command is the JSON for the newly created Product which will include an ID that looks like “prod_abc123.” We’ll use that ID to create related Price:

stripe prices create --product prod_abc123 --unit-amount 500 -d "recurring[interval]=month" --currency USD --tax-behavior=exclusive

This is the simplest way to store products and prices that we’ll use later to set up subscriptions. Starting with monthly plans is great, but we know that by also offering an annual plan, we’ll get better predictability, more cashflow, and lower accounting costs because we only need to reconcile once per year instead of monthly. The service we offer will be exactly the same, the customer will just have two options now to either pay once per month or once per year. We can persuade customers to opt for annual pricing by providing a small discount over a monthly subscription.

Now we’re faced with a decision: Create a new Price for the existing Product? Or create a new Product and a new Price for the annual plan?

The answer is: create a new Price for the existing Product. Since the feature set and value offered on the annual plan is the same as the monthly plan we should create a new Price.

stripe prices create --product prod_KcyHvtjwKmIAQx --unit-amount 5000 -d "recurring[interval]=year" --currency USD --tax-behavior=exclusive

With this single Product, we now have two price points where customers can subscribe to pay monthly or annually.

This unlocks the first benefit to proper modeling: upsells. Since we want to encourage users to purchase the annual plan, we can update the monthly Price and set it to upsell to the annual Price.

Note: Configuring upsells is not supported with the API and is dashboard only. If you’d like to use the API for upsells, we’d love to learn more about your use case, so please get in touch: @cjav_dev!

This is what customers see when they Subscribe:

As the product improves and gains traction with more sophisticated customers, we’ll need to realign the value of the offering with the price charged. One way to do this is to increase prices for all new customers. We’ll talk about how to experiment with price changes using lookup keys in a future piece. For now, we want to start offering differentiated service for our customers of different segments. We’ll create a new enterprise tier that includes dedicated support, a feature set that fits nicely with what the larger customers need.

Since this new offering will include different value for the customer, we want to create a new Product with its own Price. We also want to offer enterprise customers the option of paying monthly or annually. Let’s create a new product and its monthly and annual prices.

stripe products create --name "Business" --description "For businesses looking to achieve maximum efficiency and time savings."

stripe prices create --product prod_abcdef --unit-amount 3200 -d "recurring[interval]=month" --currency USD --tax-behavior=exclusive

stripe prices create --product prod_abcdef --unit-amount 32000 -d "recurring[interval]=year" --currency USD --tax-behavior=exclusive

One last consideration is currency. If we want to offer customers the ability to pay in a different currency than the one configured for existing prices, then we’ll need to create new Prices for the existing Products. For example, we may want to create a 28 EUR Price and a 280 EUR price for our monthly and annual plans so that our European customers can pay with EUR instead of USD.
You can follow this same pattern for a third, fourth, or fifth tier.

You can also use this fixture file with the Stripe CLI to quickly scaffold some example products and prices:

stripe fixtures value-based-saas-pricing-seed.json

In the next article, we’ll cover some common questions like how to model setup fees or mid-billing-cycle one time fees.

About the author

CJ Avilla (@cjav_dev) is a Developer Advocate at Stripe, a Ruby on Rails developer, and a YouTuber. He loves learning and teaching new programming languages and web frameworks. When he’s not at his computer, he’s spending time with his family or on a bike ride 🚲.
item.author: cjav_dev

item.title: JS中的数组旋转 | Array Rotation in JS
item.description:
大家好，今天我将向您展示如何在 JS 中旋转左一维数组旋转将取决于输入，例如 2 表示旋转。

对大小为 n 的数组的左旋转操作将数组的每个元素向左移动 1 个单位。

让我们开始吧...

 function rotateLeft ( d , arr ) { // Write your code here let newArray = [] if ( d > arr . length ){ return " Rotation value cannot be bigger than array length " } else { for ( let i = 0 ; i < d ; i ++ ){ newArray . push ( arr . shift ( i )) } let rotated = arr . concat ( newArray ) return rotated } } let array = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 , 6 ] let rotation = 2 console . log ( rotateLeft ( rotation , array ))

输出 -

在职的

  • 在函数内部，我们创建了一个空数组，它将保存旋转后的元素。
  • 之后，我们提供一个条件，我们将检查旋转次数是否大于数组长度。
  • 在 else 部分，我们使用了 for 循环直到旋转的次数（不包括，意味着如果旋转值为 4 则 for 循环将从 0-3 执行）
  • 在循环内部，我们删除了从开始到旋转值 - 1 的元素（因为数组索引从 0 开始），然后将这些删除的元素推送到我们创建的空数组中。
  • 最后，我们将原始数组与包含将在末尾删除的元素的数组组合在一起。

如果您对此代码有更好的方法或解决方案，请在评论部分提及。

谢谢你查看这篇文章^^你可以通过下面的链接捐款帮助我谢谢👇👇 ^^

还要检查这些帖子

一旦未发布，shubhamtiwari909 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 shubhamtiwari909 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 shubhamtiwari909 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 shubhamtiwari909 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Hello Guys today i will show you how to rotate left 1-d array in JS The rotation will depent on the input like 2 means rotation.

A left rotation operation on an array of size n shifts each of the array's elements 1 unit to the left.

Let's get started...

function rotateLeft(d, arr) { // Write your code here let newArray = [] if(d > arr.length){ return "Rotation value cannot be bigger than array length" } else{ for(let i=0;i<d;i++){ newArray.push(arr.shift(i)) } let rotated = arr.concat(newArray) return rotated } } let array = [1,2,3,4,5,6]
let rotation = 2
console.log(rotateLeft(rotation,array))

Output -

Working

  • Inside the function , we have created an empty array which will hold the elements after rotation.
  • After that we provide a condition where we will check if the number of rotation is greater than the array length or not.
  • In the else part , we used the for loop up to the number of rotations (exclusive, means if the rotation value is 4 then the for loop will be executed from 0-3)
  • Inside the loop we removed the element from the starting up to the rotation value - 1(because array index starts from 0) and then pushed those removed elements in the empty array we have created.
  • In the end we have combined the original array with array containing the removed elements which will come at the end.

If you have a better approach or solution to this code , please mention it in the comment section.

item.title: TypeScript 与 JavaScript🤔 | TypeScript vs JavaScript🤔
item.description:
一些开发人员更喜欢 TypeScript，因为它提供静态/强类型，而另一些开发人员更喜欢 JavaScript，因为它不太复杂。
在这篇文章中，我将讨论我对这场辩论的看法，即哪一个比另一个更好。

什么是打字稿？

根据该报告，TypeScript 已经被近 60% 的 JavaScript 开发人员使用，另外 22% 的人希望部署它。

因此，让我们看看 TypeScript 的优点和缺点，看看它是否值得添加到您自己的项目以及更大规模的项目中，以及它带来了什么。

学习和在代码中添加东西需要时间，但是如果你完成了这两件事，你会开始从中获得一些非常好的奖励，我相信这些奖励是值得的。

那么让我们来看看 TypeScript 的优点和缺点。

因此，尽管 JavaScript 是一种解释型语言，但运行时错误并不常见。

  • 出色的工具支持，包括 IntelliSense，它在添加代码时提供主动建议。

  • 通过定义模块，它具有命名空间概念。

    • 通常，TypeScript 需要时间来编译代码。
  • 如果我们想在浏览器中运行 TypeScript 应用程序，则需要一个编译步骤来将 TypeScript 转换为 JavaScript。
  • 我应该学习 JavaScript 还是 TypeScript？

    • 好吧，如果你熟悉 JavaScript，那么理解 TypeScript 对你来说会很简单。 两种语言的语法和运行时行为是相同的。

    由于其受欢迎程度，JavaScript 拥有庞大的开发社区和大量资源。 由于这两种语言经常以相同的方式使用，TypeScript 开发人员同样可以利用这些资源。

    上述问题的最佳答案当然是N​​O ！

    说到 TypeScript，它是一种完全不同的语言，只具有 JavaScript 的基本特征。 结论

    最后，在浏览了我们在本文中整理的所有见解之后，我们可以说这两种语言都有优点和缺点。

    希望编写易于理解、有条理且干净的代码的开发人员应该使用 TypeScript。 好吧，我们没有提到 TypeScript 提供了什么，包括各种实时错误检查和静态类型。

    虽然 JavaScript 不是一种综合性的编程语言，但它可以与 HTML 一起使用来提高网页的质量。 此外，您会发现许多精通 JavaScript 编码的经验丰富的开发人员。

    尽管不是功能齐全的编程语言，JavaScript 可以与 HTML 一起使用以提高网页的质量。 此外，您可能会发现许多精通 JavaScript 编码的经验丰富的工程师。

    下一步是什么？



    原文:

    TypeScript vs JavaScript is one of the most controversial topics in tech.
    Some developers prefer TypeScript because it offers static/strong typing, while others prefer JavaScript because it is less complex.
    In this article, I will discuss my thoughts on this debate of which one is better than the other.

    What is TypeScript?

    TypeScript is an open-source programming language for developing large-level applications. TypeScript was developed by one of the tech giants - Microsoft, in 2012. The actual reason behind this TypeScript development was to handle large-scale applications.

    TypeScript is also used by Angular, a JavaScript framework, for web development services. According to the report, TypeScript is already used by almost 60% of JavaScript developers, and another 22% want to deploy it.

    So let's look at the advantages and drawbacks of TypeScript and see if it's worth adding not just on your own projects but also on bigger scale projects, and what it brings to the table.

    TypeScript is a long-term investment; it takes time to learn and time to add things to your code, but if you accomplish both of those things, you'll start getting some very nice rewards from it, and I believe these rewards are worthwhile.

    So lets take a look at the Advantages and Drawbacks of TypeScript.

    Advantage of TypeScript over JavaScript

    1. TypeScript constantly highlights compilation errors throughout development. As a result, although JavaScript is an interpreted language, runtime errors are less common.

    2. TypeScript supports static/strong typing, whereas this is not in JavaScript.

    3. TypeScript runs on any browser or JavaScript engine.

    4. Excellent tools support, including IntelliSense, which offers active suggestions as code is added.

    5. It has a namespace concept by defining a module.

    Drawbacks of TypeScript over JavaScript

    1. Generally, TypeScript takes time to compile the code.
    2. A compilation step is necessary to convert TypeScript into JavaScript if we want to run the TypeScript application in the browser.
    3. TypeScript does not support abstract classes.

    Should I learn JavaScript or TypeScript?

    Well, understanding TypeScript will be simple for you if you are familiar with JavaScript. The syntax and run-time behavior of both languages are identical.

    Due to its popularity, JavaScript has a large development community and a lot of resources. Because both languages are frequently used in the same way, TypeScript developers may likewise take advantage of such resources.

    Will TypeScript Replace JavaScript?

    The best answer to the above question is NO, of course!

    Speaking of TypeScript, it is a totally distinct language that only shares JavaScript's fundamental characteristics. JavaScript cannot and will not ever be replaced.

    Conclusion

    In the end, after going through all the insights we have curated in this article, we can say that both languages have advantages and drawbacks.

    Developers that wish to write understandable, organised, and clean code should use TypeScript. Well, we are not mentioning what TypeScript offers are, including various live-bugs checking, and static typing.

    Although JavaScript is not a comprehensive programming language, it can be used with HTML to improve the quality of web pages. Moreover, you will find many experienced developers who are proficient in JavaScript coding.

    Despite not being a full-featured programming language, JavaScript may be used alongside HTML to enhance the quality of web pages. Additionally, you may discover a lot of seasoned engineers that are skilled at coding in JavaScript.

    What’s next?
item.author: iarchitsharma

item.title: 如何为最近的 JDK 版本配置 JitPack | How to Configure JitPack for Recent JDK Versions
item.description:
我打算将此作为系列文章的第一篇，其中包含与有效使用 Maven 存储库进行依赖管理、工件存储等相关的各种提示和技巧，适用于 Java 和其他 JDK 语言（如 Kotlin）。 出于各种原因，我首选的 Maven 存储库当然是 Maven 中央存储库，并且我可能会在本系列的后面包含一些关于 Maven 中央的帖子。

然而，现在，我从一个与JitPack相关的技巧开始，特别是如果您的项目需要最新的 JDK 版本（例如，JDK 版本 > 8），或者如果您想使用特定的 JDK 发行版构建，如何配置 JitPack 构建。 我不隶属于 JitPack，这篇文章是基于我最近在我的几个项目中配置 JitPack 构建的经验，其中 JitPack 文档没有完全满足我的要求。

目录：这篇文章的其余部分组织如下：

  • 关于 JitPack

    对于大多数 Maven 存储库，例如 Maven Central 或 GitHub Packages，您首先在您自己的系统上本地构建工件，或者可能作为 CI/CD 工作流程的一部分（例如，通过 GitHub Actions）； 然后在构建您的工件（例如，编译的类文件、源代码、文档等的各种 jar 文件）之后，您将它们部署到您选择的 Maven 工件存储库。

    相反，JitPack 旨在直接从托管在 GitHub 或任何其他 git 主机（例如 GitLab、Bitbucket 等）上的源代码存储库按需构建工件。按需构建在第一次请求工件版本时发生，然后简单地送达。

    为什么选择 JitPack？

    我已经将我维护的所有 Java 库的工件发布到 Maven Central，可以说是权威的 Maven 存储库，那么为什么还要发布到 JitPack？ 您不想在生产中依赖其中之一。 但是，在开发过程中，有时针对非常特定的未发布版本的依赖项进行构建可能会很有用。 使用 JitPack，您可以通过将提交的哈希指定为版本，甚至指定分支来做到这一点。 这不就像使用 SNAPSHOT 构建一样吗？ 是的，但在这种情况下，没有理由创建您可能需要或不需要的 SNAPSHOT 构建。 或者您的 SNAPSHOT 可能是每晚构建的，但是您发现需要针对每晚构建之间发生的 git 历史记录中的特定点进行构建。 或者，您的夜间快照可能是从默认分支构建的，并且您希望针对功能分支构建依赖项，该功能分支是您也在处理的依赖项中正在进行的工作。

    这就是我开始将工件发布到 JitPack 以及 Maven Central 的原因。 本质上，作为任何和所有提交的 SNAPSHOT 构建源，以及任何和所有分支，包括短期拉请求分支，我都没有任何干预。

    但是等等，你刚才是不是说“开始向 JitPack 发布工件”？ 我以为你说无论存储库的维护者是否在任何地方发布工件，JitPack 都会处理所有这些？ 我确实说过，这是真的，前提是 JitPack 可以找到构建文件（例如 Maven pom.xml 或 Gradle 等效文件），并且您的项目可以使用 JitPack 默认值构建。 如何指定 JitPack 依赖项

    如何指定依赖项的详细信息取决于您的构建工具。 我使用 Maven，所以我的示例将使用 Maven。 默认情况下，Maven 在 Maven 中央存储库中搜索依赖项的工件。 如果要从其他任何地方导入依赖项，则需要将详细信息添加到 pom.xml 的<repositories></repositories>部分。 要从 JitPack 导入，请将以下内容插入 pom.xml 的该部分（如果您当前仅从 Maven Central 导入，则可能需要创建这样的部分）：

     <repository> <id> jitpack.io </id> <url> https://jitpack.io </url> </repository>

    要从 Maven 存储库中导入依赖项，您需要指定其坐标，包括其groupId 、 artifactId和version 。 例如，在 Maven Central 和 GitHub Packages 上，我使用org.cicirello作为我所有 Java 库的groupId 。 然而，尽管 JitPack 确实支持这样的自定义 groupId，但 JitPack 的groupId通常由 git 主机的域以及拥有您要导入的存储库的用户名或组织名称组成。 对于 JitPack， artifactId是存储库的名称。 我在这里的示例将假设您将 GitHub 存储库指定为依赖项，但对于其他主机（如 Bitbucket），您需要非常相似的东西。

    要使用标签导入，例如v1.2.3之类的发布标签，请将以下内容插入 pom.xml 的<dependencies></dependencies>部分：

     <dependency> <groupId> com.github.USERNAME </groupId> <artifactId> REPOSITORY_NAME </artifactId> <version> v1.2.3 </version> </dependency>

    或者，如果您想针对特定分支的当前状态（可能是“主”）进行构建，您可以使用：

     <dependency> <groupId> com.github.USERNAME </groupId> <artifactId> REPOSITORY_NAME </artifactId> <version> main-SNAPSHOT </version> </dependency>

    甚至是特定的提交哈希：

     <dependency> <groupId> com.github.USERNAME </groupId> <artifactId> REPOSITORY_NAME </artifactId> <version> COMMIT_HASH_GOES_HERE </version> </dependency>

    无论您使用上述哪种方法，请注意，如果您要导入的版本以前没有构建过，那么在 Maven（或 Gradle 或您正在使用的任何构建工具）等待来自 JitPack 的响应时，您的构建将会有延迟，因为JitPack 必须在交付工件之前首先构建依赖项。 任何后续进口，无论是您还是其他人，都不会延迟。

    以下是与上述等效的示例，但使用我的一个存储库，用于我维护的随机本地搜索和进化算法的 Java 库Chips-n-Salsa 。

    带标签：

     <dependency> <groupId> com.github.cicirello </groupId> <artifactId> chips-n-salsa </artifactId> <version> v5.2.0 </version> </dependency>

    从标签中删除 v ：

     <dependency> <groupId> com.github.cicirello </groupId> <artifactId> chips-n-salsa </artifactId> <version> 5.2.0 </version> </dependency>

    默认分支中的最新提交：

     <dependency> <groupId> com.github.cicirello </groupId> <artifactId> chips-n-salsa </artifactId> <version> master-SNAPSHOT </version> </dependency>

    从提交哈希：

     <dependency> <groupId> com.github.cicirello </groupId> <artifactId> chips-n-salsa </artifactId> <version> 278803985ceb0e3d1515eefb2a69d0601e0176cf </version> </dependency>

    来自拉取请求：

     <dependency> <groupId> com.github.cicirello </groupId> <artifactId> chips-n-salsa </artifactId> <version> PR451-SNAPSHOT </version> </dependency>

    我还在 JitPack 上配置了我的反向域，因此除了<groupId>org.cicirello</groupId>之外，上述所有内容同样适用于<groupId>com.github.cicirello</groupId> ，这有助于保持一致性与 Maven 中心。 但只有已发布的版本可以通过 Maven Central 获得，例如通过以下依赖项：

     <dependency> <groupId> org.cicirello </groupId> <artifactId> chips-n-salsa </artifactId> <version> 5.2.0 </version> </dependency>

    如何配置 JitPack 构建

    您可能需要也可能根本不需要配置 JitPack。 如果您的存储库的根目录中有一个 Maven pom.xml（或 Gradle 的任何等效项），并且如果您的项目可以使用 Java 8 构建，那么您可能不需要任何配置。 您或其他任何人都可以使用 JitPack 将您的存储库作为 Maven 依赖项导入。 在存储库的根目录中找到 pom.xml 后，JitPack 将尝试使用 Java 8 进行构建，其中包含以下内容：

    我想它会跳过运行您的测试以减少第一次请求工件时的延迟时间。 这似乎是合理的。

    就我而言，我的大多数库都需要 Java 17，因此如果没有配置，JitPack 构建会失败。 要配置更新的 JDK，请执行以下步骤。

    第 1 步：在存储库的根目录中创建一个名为jitpack.yml的文件。 这是所有配置发生的地方。 请随时查阅本文所基于的项目的jitpack.yml ，了解完整的详细信息。

    第 2 步：在jitpack.yml中指定 JDK 版本，例如：

    请注意，如果您猜测（像我一样）以上内容就足够了，那您就错了。 您现在需要安装要使用的 JDK 发行版，并明确指定要使用它。 我在本文所基于的项目中使用 OpenJDK 的 Temurin 发行版。 您需要我在下面添加的两个语句。 如果不使用sdk use ，JitPack 将继续使用 Java 8。您可以在 sdkman 站点上找到可用 JDK 的完整列表。

     jdk : - openjdk17 before_install : - sdk install java 17.0.3-tem - sdk use java 17.0.3-tem

    第 3 步：更新 Maven。 在某些情况下，上述内容可能就足够了。 对我来说，事实并非如此。 如果您正在使用它（就像我一样），除非您更新 Maven，否则您的 JitPack 构建将失败。 您可以通过将上述内容修改为以下内容来做到这一点（在撰写本文时会将 Maven 更新到 3.8.6）：

     jdk : - openjdk17 before_install : - sdk install java 17.0.3-tem - sdk use java 17.0.3-tem - sdk install maven - mvn -v

    你真的不需要上面的mvn -v 。 我将其包括在内以在构建日志中显示版本。

    第 4 步：自定义安装步骤。 如果 JitPack 的默认构建命令mvn install -DskipTests与您的项目相关，您可能已经完成了。 就我而言，我还想禁用 JitPack 的一项功能。 许多人可能喜欢的 JitPack 功能之一是，如果构建生成 javadocs 的 jar，JitPack 将自动提取 javadocs 并提供它们，包括维护每个版本的 javadocs。 如果他们只是像 Maven Central 那样提供 javadocs 的 jar，而不是自动托管它们，我会更喜欢它。 对于我维护的库，我还在项目站点上托管 javadocs，如果 javadocs 的最终版本明确地是托管在我的域cicirello.org上的版本，我会更喜欢。 因此，对于 JitPack 构建，我只是禁用 javadoc 生成，导致下面的完整jitpack.yml ：

     jdk : - openjdk17 before_install : - sdk install java 17.0.3-tem - sdk use java 17.0.3-tem - sdk install maven - mvn -v install : - mvn install -Dmaven.javadoc.skip=true -DskipTests

    重要提示：由于 JitPack 本质上构建存储库快照的方式，即使对于发布版本， jitpack.yml必须存在于存储库的特定快照中才能应用。 例如，对于本文所基于的库Chips-n-Salsa ，存储库包含配置文件的第一个版本是 5.0.1，因此 JitPack 将无法构建任何以前的版本，尽管早期版本可以通过Maven 中心和 GitHub 包。 同样，如果您在引入该配置文件的提交之前指定任何提交哈希，则 JitPack 构建也将失败。

    实时配置示例：请参阅此jitpack.yml以获取完整且实时的示例，该示例可在以下存储库中找到：

    版权所有 (C) 2002-2022文森特 A. Cicirello 。

    网站： https ://chips-n-salsa.cicirello.org/

    关于图书馆的出版物 软件包和版本 构建状态 安全 执照 支持

    如何引用

    如果您在研究中使用此库，请引用以下论文：

    弗吉尼亚州西西雷洛 (2020)。 开源软件杂志，5(52)，2448， https: //doi.org/10.21105/joss.02448。

    概述

    该库包括几种随机局部搜索算法的实现，包括模拟退火、爬山算法以及随机采样等建设性搜索算法。 该库非常广泛地支持模拟…

在哪里可以找到我

在网上：

卡内基梅隆大学机器人学博士。 他是 ACM 高级会员、IEEE 高级会员、AAAI 终身会员、EAI 杰出会员和 SIAM 会员。

在 DEV 上关注我：

在 GitHub 上关注我：

可以找到我或我的作品的网站
网络和社交媒体
软件开发
出版物

如果您想为您自己的 GitHub 配置文件生成与上述等效的内容，请查看cicirello/user-statistician GitHub 操作。






原文:

I intend for this to be the first post of a series with various tips and tricks related to effective use of Maven repositories for dependency management, artifact storage, etc, for Java and other JDK languages such as Kotlin. My preferred Maven repository is of course the Maven Central Repository for a variety of reasons, and I will likely include some posts on Maven Central later in the series.

For now, however, I am beginning with a tip related to JitPack, specifically how to configure JitPack builds if your project requires recent JDK versions (e.g., JDK version > 8), or if you want to build with a specific JDK distribution. I am not affiliated with JitPack, and this post is based on my recent experience configuring JitPack builds in a couple of my projects where the JitPack documentation didn't entirely cover my requirements.

Table of Contents: The rest of this post is organized as follows:

  • About JitPack: I need to start with an explanation of how JitPack works because it is a bit different than other Maven repositories.
  • Why JitPack?: Brief explanation of why I've begun making my libraries available via JitPack in addition to other repositories.
  • How to Specify a JitPack Dependency: Although not the purpose of this post, it will be useful to explain how to specify a dependency on an artifact served from JitPack.
  • How to Configure JitPack Builds: Explanation with examples of configuring JitPack builds, including an example in a live project on GitHub.
  • Where you can find me

About JitPack

JitPack works a bit differently than most other Maven artifact repositories. For most Maven repositories, such as Maven Central or GitHub Packages, you first build the artifacts either locally on your own system, or perhaps as part of a CI/CD workflow (e.g., via GitHub Actions); and then after building your artifacts (e.g., various jar files of compiled class files, source code, documentation, etc) you deploy them to your chosen Maven artifact repository.

JitPack doesn't work like this. Instead, JitPack is designed to build artifacts on-demand direct from a source code repository hosted on GitHub or any other git host, such as GitLab, Bitbucket, etc. The on-demand build occurs the first time a version of an artifact is requested, and then simply served thereafter.

One unique aspect of JitPack that directly derives from its approach is that developers can include dependencies on any public git repository on GitHub, Bitbucket, and other git hosts, even if the maintainers of those repositories don't explicitly publish artifacts to any Maven repository.

Why JitPack?

I am already publishing artifacts of all of the Java libraries I maintain to Maven Central, arguably the definitive Maven repository, so why do I also publish to JitPack? JitPack enables specifying any git tag, branch, pull request, or commit hash as the version for a dependency. You wouldn't want to rely on one of these in production. However, during development at times it might be useful to build against a very specific unreleased version of a dependency. With JitPack, you can do this by specifying the hash of the commit as the version, or even specifying a branch. Isn't this just like using a SNAPSHOT build? Yes, but in this case there is no reason to create SNAPSHOT builds that you may or may not need. Or perhaps your SNAPSHOTs are built nightly, but you find a need to build against a specific point in the git history that occurred between the nightly builds. Or perhaps your nightly SNAPSHOTs are built from the default branch, and you want to build a dependent against a feature branch that is a work-in-progress in the dependency that you are also working on.

This is essentially why I've started publishing artifacts to JitPack in addition to Maven Central. Essentially, as a source of SNAPSHOT builds for any and all commits, and for any and all branches including short-lived pull-request branches, without any intervention on my part.

But wait, did you just say "started publishing artifacts to JitPack"? I thought you said JitPack handles all of this regardless of whether the maintainer of a repository publishes artifacts anywhere? I did say that, and it is true, provided that JitPack can find a build file (such as a Maven pom.xml or the Gradle equivalent), and provided that your project can build with the JitPack defaults. We'll get to this in a bit....

How to Specify a JitPack Dependency

The details of how to specify dependencies depends upon your build tool. I use Maven, so my examples will use Maven. By default, Maven searches for artifacts of dependencies in the Maven Central Repository. If you want to import dependencies from anywhere else, you need to add the details to the <repositories></repositories> section of your pom.xml. To import from JitPack, insert the following into that section of your pom.xml (you might need to create such a section if you are currently importing only from Maven Central):

<repository> <id>jitpack.io</id> <url>https://jitpack.io</url>
</repository>

To import a dependency from a Maven repository, you need to specify its coordinates, which consists in its groupId, its artifactId, and its version. The groupId is generally a reverse domain, or subdomain, controlled by the one publishing the artifacts. For example, on Maven Central and GitHub Packages, I use org.cicirello as the groupId for all of my Java libraries. However, although JitPack does support such custom groupIds, the groupId for JitPack is usually formed from the domain of the git host along with the username or organization name who owns the repository that you want to import. The artifactId is the name of the artifact, which is sometimes the name of a package or module within it, but it is not required to be. For JitPack, artifactId is the name of the repository. And version is, well, the version that you want.

My examples here will assume you are specifying a GitHub repository as a dependency, but you'll need something very similar for other hosts like Bitbucket.

To import using a tag, such as a release tag like v1.2.3 insert the following into the <dependencies></dependencies> section of your pom.xml:

<dependency> <groupId>com.github.USERNAME</groupId> <artifactId>REPOSITORY_NAME</artifactId> <version>v1.2.3</version>
</dependency>

JitPack also automatically supports dropping the v, so if you want to be consistent with other Maven repositories, you can also use:

<dependency> <groupId>com.github.USERNAME</groupId> <artifactId>REPOSITORY_NAME</artifactId> <version>1.2.3</version>
</dependency>

Or if you want to build against the current state of a specific branch, perhaps "main", you can use:

<dependency> <groupId>com.github.USERNAME</groupId> <artifactId>REPOSITORY_NAME</artifactId> <version>main-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>

Or even a specific commit hash with:

<dependency> <groupId>com.github.USERNAME</groupId> <artifactId>REPOSITORY_NAME</artifactId> <version>COMMIT_HASH_GOES_HERE</version>
</dependency>

Whichever of the above you use, be aware that if the version you are importing has not previously been built, there will be a delay in your build while Maven (or Gradle or whatever build tool you are using) waits for a response from JitPack since JitPack must first build the dependency before delivering artifacts. Any subsequent imports, whether by you or someone else, will be without such delay.

Here are the equivalent examples as above, but using one of my repositories, for a Java library I maintain of stochastic local search and evolutionary algorithms, Chips-n-Salsa.

With tag:

<dependency> <groupId>com.github.cicirello</groupId> <artifactId>chips-n-salsa</artifactId> <version>v5.2.0</version>
</dependency>

Dropping the v from the tag:

<dependency> <groupId>com.github.cicirello</groupId> <artifactId>chips-n-salsa</artifactId> <version>5.2.0</version>
</dependency>

Latest commit in default branch:

<dependency> <groupId>com.github.cicirello</groupId> <artifactId>chips-n-salsa</artifactId> <version>master-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>

From a commit hash:

<dependency> <groupId>com.github.cicirello</groupId> <artifactId>chips-n-salsa</artifactId> <version>278803985ceb0e3d1515eefb2a69d0601e0176cf</version>
</dependency>

From a pull-request:

<dependency> <groupId>com.github.cicirello</groupId> <artifactId>chips-n-salsa</artifactId> <version>PR451-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>

JitPack also supports configuring reverse domain:

I have also configured my reverse domain on JitPack, so all of the above will likewise work with <groupId>org.cicirello</groupId> in addition to <groupId>com.github.cicirello</groupId>, which is nice for consistency with Maven Central. But only the released versions are available via Maven Central, such as through a dependency like:

<dependency> <groupId>org.cicirello</groupId> <artifactId>chips-n-salsa</artifactId> <version>5.2.0</version>
</dependency>

How to Configure JitPack Builds

You may or may not need to configure JitPack at all. If you have a Maven pom.xml in the root of your repository (or whatever the equivalent of this is for Gradle), AND if your project can be built with Java 8, then you likely won't need any configuration. You, or anyone else for that matter, can already utilize JitPack to import your repository as a Maven dependency. Upon finding a pom.xml at the root of your repository, JitPack will attempt to build using Java 8 with the following:

I imagine that it skips running your tests to decrease the length of the delay the first time an artifact is requested. This seems reasonable.

In my case, most of my libraries require Java 17, so without configuration JitPack builds fail. To configure for a more recent JDK, here are the steps.

Step 1: Create a file named jitpack.yml at the root of your repository. This is where all configuration takes place. Feel free to consult the jitpack.yml for the project this post is based upon, Chips-n-Salsa, for full details.

Step 2: Specify the JDK version within jitpack.yml such as with:

Note that if you were to guess (like I did) that the above is sufficient, you'd be incorrect. You now need to install the JDK distribution that you want to use, as well as explicitly specify that you want to use it. I'm using the Temurin distribution of OpenJDK in the project that this post is based upon. You need both of the statements I've added below. Without the sdk use, JitPack will continue to use Java 8. You can find the full list of available JDKs on the sdkman site.

jdk: - openjdk17
before_install: - sdk install java 17.0.3-tem - sdk use java 17.0.3-tem

Step 3: Update Maven. The above might be sufficient in some cases. For me, it was not. JitPack currently (as of the time of the writing of this post) has Maven 3.6.1 installed. Maven 3.6.1 was released over 3 years ago in April 2019. The <release> property for configuring Java version didn't exist then. If you are using that (as I am), your JitPack build will fail unless you update Maven. You can do that by revising the above to what follows (which as of the writing of this post will update Maven to 3.8.6):

jdk: - openjdk17
before_install: - sdk install java 17.0.3-tem - sdk use java 17.0.3-tem - sdk install maven - mvn -v

You don't really need the mvn -v above. I included that to show the version in the build logs.

Step 4: Customize the install step. You might be done at this point, provided that JitPack's default build command of mvn install -DskipTests is relevant to your project. In my case, I wanted to also disable one of JitPack's features. One of JitPack's features, which many probably like, is that if the build produces a jar of the javadocs, JitPack will automatically extract the javadocs and serve them, including maintaining the javadocs for each release. I would prefer it if they simply served the jar of the javadocs, as Maven Central does, rather than automatically hosting them. For the libraries that I maintain, I also host the javadocs on the project's site, and would prefer if the definitive version of the javadocs is unambiguously the version hosted on my domain, cicirello.org. Thus, for JitPack builds, I simply disable javadoc generation, leading to the complete jitpack.yml below:

jdk: - openjdk17
before_install: - sdk install java 17.0.3-tem - sdk use java 17.0.3-tem - sdk install maven - mvn -v
install: - mvn install -Dmaven.javadoc.skip=true -DskipTests

Important Note: Because of the way JitPack essentially builds a snapshot of your repository, even for release builds, the jitpack.yml must be present within the specific snapshot of your repository in order to apply. For example, for the library that this post is based upon, Chips-n-Salsa, the first release where the repository contains the configuration file is 5.0.1, so JitPack will fail to build any prior version, although earlier versions are available via Maven Central and GitHub Packages. Likewise, if you specify any commit hash prior to that of the commit where that configuration file was introduced, the JitPack build will also fail.

Live Configuration Example: See this jitpack.yml for a complete and live example, which is found within the following repository:

Copyright (C) 2002-2022 Vincent A. Cicirello.

Website: https://chips-n-salsa.cicirello.org/

API documentation: https://chips-n-salsa.cicirello.org/api/

Publications About the Library
Packages and Releases
Build Status
JaCoCo Test Coverage
Security
DOI
License
Support

How to Cite

If you use this library in your research, please cite the following paper:

Cicirello, V. A., (2020). Chips-n-Salsa: A Java Library of Customizable, Hybridizable, Iterative, Parallel, Stochastic, and Self-Adaptive Local Search Algorithms. Journal of Open Source Software, 5(52), 2448, https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.02448 .

Overview

Chips-n-Salsa is a Java library of customizable, hybridizable, iterative, parallel, stochastic, and self-adaptive local search algorithms. The library includes implementations of several stochastic local search algorithms, including simulated annealing, hill climbers, as well as constructive search algorithms such as stochastic sampling. Chips-n-Salsa now also includes genetic algorithms as well as evolutionary algorithms more generally. The library very extensively supports simulated…

Where you can find me

On the Web:

Vincent A. Cicirello - Professor of Computer Science at Stockton University - is a researcher in artificial intelligence, evolutionary computation, swarm intelligence, and computational intelligence, with a Ph.D. in Robotics from Carnegie Mellon University. He is an ACM Senior Member, IEEE Senior Member, AAAI Life Member, EAI Distinguished Member, and SIAM Member.

Follow me here on DEV:

Follow me on GitHub:

Sites where you can find me or my work
Web and social media
Software development
Publications

If you want to generate the equivalent to the above for your own GitHub profile, check out the cicirello/user-statistician GitHub Action.
item.author: cicirello

item.title: useEffect 的问题 | Issues with useEffect
item.description:
拉詹拉加

发表于如果你是 react 开发者，你肯定会遇到 useEffect 的多个用例。 但是让我展示一下 useEffect 是如何一直在欺骗你的。

您希望在组件安装时运行某些功能

useEffect(() => ...fn() )

这里一切都很好。

但是现在您希望函数在某些特定状态值发生更改时运行

 const [data, setData] = useState(""); useEffect(() => { console.log("Running useEffect"); }, [data]);

我们的期望是这个函数会在数据改变时运行。

检查这个小例子。 在这里，我们可以看到即使我不更改数据，useEffect 回调也会运行。
那是因为 useEffect 将在组件挂载时至少运行一次。

那么这里有什么解决方法？
我们都使用它来进行一些 api 调用并执行一些关键工作，并且由于 useEffect 的这种行为，我们可能会面临错误或性能问题。

因此，只有在数据更改时才运行它，我们可以。

 import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from "react"; export function App() { const isMounted = useRef(false); const [data, setData] = useState(""); useEffect(() => { if (isMounted.current) { console.log("HERE"); } else { isMounted.current = true; } }, [data]); return ( <div> <input onChange={(e) => setData(e.target.value)} placeholder="First name" /> </div> ); }

现在在控制台中，您不会在安装时看到 HERE。

请注意，这里我们专门使用了 useRef，以便在重新渲染 isMounted 的值时坚持 else 使用简单的 js 变量，它将在每次安装时保持重置。

如果你学到了一些很棒的东西，那就打吧。

一旦暂停，rajanlagah 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，rajanlagah 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，rajanlagah 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 rajanlagah 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 rajanlagah 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 rajanlagah 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Rajan lagah

Posted on Aug 4

If you are react dev you surely encounter multiple use cases for useEffect. But let me show how useEffect is tricking you all these times.

You want some function to run when component mounts

useEffect(() => ...fn() )

Everything is good here.

But now you want function to run when some specific state value is changed

 const [data, setData] = useState("");
 useEffect(() => {
    console.log("Running useEffect");
  }, [data]);

our expectation is that this function will run when data is changed.

Check this small example. Here we can see that useEffect callback ran even if I don't change data.
That is because useEffect will run at least once on component mount.

So what is fix here?
We all use this to make some api calls and do some critical work and due to this behaviour of useEffect we can face bugs or performance issue.

So to run it only when data is changed we can.

import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from "react";

export function App() {
  const isMounted = useRef(false);
  const [data, setData] = useState("");
  useEffect(() => {
    if (isMounted.current) {
      console.log("HERE");
    } else {
      isMounted.current = true;
    }
  }, [data]);
  return (
    <div>
      <input
        onChange={(e) => setData(e.target.value)}
        placeholder="First name"
      />
    </div>
  );
}

Now in the console you will not see HERE on mount.

Notice here we are using useRef specifically so that on re-render value of isMounted persist else with simple js variable it will keep reseting on every mount.

Hit like if you have learned something awesome.

AakritiGoyal12458 - Aug 1

Zoltán Szőgyényi - Jul 31

item.title: DevOps 对公司的好处 | The benefits of DevOps for companies
item.description:
让我们仔细看看采用 DevOps 方法可以为您的业务带来的主要优势。

规划和组织效益

批次大小。 与传统的 SDLC 不同，DevOps 与快速和频繁的交付以及更短的开发时间相关联。 持续交付和集成对于实现 DevOps 目标至关重要。 允许少量交付和快速反馈周期的 DevOps 方法。 根据需要进行软件修改以进行修改。 实施 DevOps 提高了应用程序更新到生产的频率，这对软件开发过程产生了积极影响。 较小的批次更易于管理，风险更小，并且更易于管理和测试。

专门的团队，而不是基于技能的孤岛。 使用传统的 SDLC，有必要将所有细节都传达给您的项目经理，然后随时请求允许部署您需要的项目。 这可能是一个非常耗时的过程。 借助 DevOps，可以在适当的时间做出决策，并计划在最佳时间启动该系统。 曾经孤立的部门现在可以在公司的一个部门与下一个部门之间进行沟通，而无需等待很长时间。 增加的可见性和透明度使组织更高效，对您的团队成员的干扰更少，这使得一个工程师团队能够在整个应用程序生命周期中工作，从而增强他们的能力。

文化和表演相关的好处

改进协作和沟通： DevOps 意味着文化的巨大转变，它不仅消除了沟通障碍，还允许员工在共享环境中轻松协作。 此外，最有效的 DevOps 参与者是那些意识到协作重要性的人。 为了让 DevOps 运营取得成功，所有相关人员都必须参与其中。 不同团队的协调使他们都可以合作，可以防止相互指责，促进信任与合作。 想象一下，有多少问题可以由团队独立工作来解决，而不是依赖于官方的运营链顺序。

缩短恢复时间： DevOps 实践特别适用于减少回滚、瓶颈和部署问题对整个流程效率的影响。 当错误确实发生时，修复这些错误所需的时间更少。 如果开发周期被缩短并且新版本的发布频繁，那么发现问题所需的平均时间也会大大减少。 更早地检测代码中最小的错误变得更加容易。 更准确的错误检测会产生更有效的反馈，一方面连接开发和运营团队，另一方面连接用户。

提高效率：自动化是 DevOps 文化的核心，因为它有助于确保 DevOps 的所有其他方面也得到遵守。 通常，开发环境是手工构建的，但 DevOps 环境也可以通过自动化流程来构建，以管理产品不同工作负载的快速开发，以及随着时间的推移不断变化的需求。 但是，不要忘记该过程通常涉及许多变量。 建议采用增量方法在组织内引入 DevOps。 如果您有兴趣发展蓬勃发展的 DevOps 职业，现在是时候接受DevOps 培训，了解这些越来越有价值的技能。

战略和运营优势
以最小风险实现稳定性和规模：确保高压时期的可靠性至关重要。 然而，当流程和系统受到越来越大的压力时，它们可能会变得具有挑战性。 这就是为什么实施专注于可扩展性、可靠性和确保持续增长的有效战略至关重要的原因。 这是一种敏捷方法，可提高软件的多功能性和可靠性。 因此，团队在对产品进行更新和修改时将能够确保获得最佳用户体验。

降低成本： DevOps 计划不仅可以创造增加业务价值的创新，还可以降低维护和升级成本，减少不必要的资本支出，降低项目成本以及预算超支。 当实施完整的 DevOps 计划时，公司可以大幅削减成本并提高利润。

特别是，您可以从集成到测试和生产管道中的规模经济中受益。 通过自动化发布管道，DevOps 不仅可以让您更快地发布，而且还可以降低所需的人力，从而降低成本。 在许多情况下，这可能意味着减少与部署相关的一些成本，以及能够减少停机时间和变更控制措施的成本。



原文:

Let's take an close look at the main advantages to your business you could gain by embracing DevOps methods.

Planning and organizational benefits

Sizes of batches. Unlike traditional SDLC, DevOps is associated with rapid and frequent delivery, as well as shorter development times. Continuous delivery and integration are crucial to achieve DevOps goals . DevOps methods that permit small amounts of delivery and quick feedback cycles. Software modifications are made as necessary to make them. Implementing DevOps improves the frequency of app updates to production, which positively impacts how software development process. Smaller batches are simpler to manage, less risky, and easier to manage and test.

dedicated teams rather than silos that are based on skill. With traditional SDLC it is necessary to communicate everything in detail to the managers of your projects, and then ask permission to deploy the items you need at any time. This can be an extremely time-consuming procedure. With DevOps it is possible to make decisions at the appropriate time and plan to launch this system during the perfect time. Once siloed departments could now communicate without having to wait a long time between one section of the company to the following. The increased visibility and transparency make the organization more efficient and less disruptive to your team members, which allows one team of engineers to be able to work throughout the app lifecycle, thereby enhancing their capabilities.

Cultural and performance-related benefits

Improved collaboration and communication: DevOps means a huge shift in culture, one which not only eliminates obstacles in communication but also permits employees to collaborate easily in a shared environment. In addition, the most effective DevOps participants are the ones who appreciate the importance of collaboration. In order for DevOps operations to be successful everyone involved must be engaged. Coordination of different teams so that they can all be cooperative can prevent the blame game, and promote trust and cooperation. Imagine how many problems can be solved by teams working independently, rather than relying on an official order of operations chain.

Reduced recovery time: DevOps practices are specifically suited to reduce the effects of rollbacks, bottlenecks and deployment issues on efficiency of the overall process. When errors do occur regardless, less time is required to repair these. If development cycles are cut down and the release of new versions is frequently, the average time required to spot a problem is drastically decreased, as well. It becomes easier to detect the smallest bugs in the code earlier. More accurate error detection creates more efficient feedback that connects development and operations teams on one hand and the users on the other.

Improved efficiency: Automation is at the heart of DevOps culture since it assists in ensuring that all the other aspects of DevOps are adhered to, too. Typically the development environment is built by hand, but an DevOps environments can also be built by automating processes to manage the rapid development of the product different workloads, and evolving demands in the course of time. The speed of which DevOps solutions are implemented is vitally important. But, don't forget that the process is typically involving numerous variables. It's recommended to employ an incremental method of the introduction of DevOps within an organization. If you are interested in developing a flourishing DevOps career, the time is now to get DevOps Training in these increasingly valuable skills.

Strategic and operational advantages
Stability and scale at a minimum risk: Ensuring reliability during high-pressure times is vital. However, when processes and systems are subject to increased pressure they can become challenging. This is why implementing an effective strategy that is focused on scalability, reliability, and ensuring that the growth continues is crucial. DevOps allows teams to quickly develop new software and protect the data on premises that is already in a distributed system. It's an agile method which increases the versatility and reliability of your software. DevOps practices focus on the coordination of the team's work while preserving security of your development environment. Thus, teams will be able to ensure the best user experience as they make updates and modifications to the product.

Cost reductions: DevOps initiatives not just create innovations that add the value of a business, but can also reduce the cost of maintenance and upgrades, reducing unnecessary capital expenditures and reducing the cost of projects as well as budget overruns. When there is a complete DevOps plan implemented, a company can drastically cut costs and boost its profit.

Particularly, you can benefit from economies of scale integrated into test and production pipelines. Through automating release pipelines DevOps can not only allow you to release faster , but it also lowers the amount of manpower required and thus reduces the cost. In many instances this could mean reducing some of the cost involved with deployments as well being able to reduce downtime and the cost of changes control measures.

item.title: 莫西尼奥？ Vamos entender! Vamos 深情！ | Método construtor em Java, Vilão? Mocinho? Vamos entender!
item.description:
验证对象。

做 código。

没有对象的过程。

变量=勇气

瓯

类类 = 新类（）；

阿西姆…… 费周？ Papo meio denso mas te espero na próxima. Papo meio denso mas te espero na próxima。



原文:

Quem são os construtores de classe em Java?

Construtores de classe no Java existem de forma implícita, ou seja, naturalmente eles são gerados, mesmo que não haja declaração por parte de nós “devs”, a mãezona JVM trata de criar eles, isso por que precisa ser alocado um espaço na memória para esse novo objeto que você acabou de dar um new, podemos entender assim, o construtor executa operações necessárias para inicializar a classe antes que os métodos sejam invocados ou os campos sejam acessados,( penso até que poderíamos pensar em um construtor como um inicializador ***), os atributos ganham valores padrões, **ex: atributos do tipo String ganham valor null, primitivos do tipo número ganham valor 0.0, os construtores(constructors) são usados para gerar atomicidade, ou seja, ou cria o objeto em um estado válido, ou não cria, dessa forma garante-se que para aquele objeto tenha sempre um padrão “criacional”( nem sei se essa palavra existe rsrs), mas você entendeu certo?

Além do mais, em alguns casos o código fica mais (“ninitinho”) elegante(clean), outra coisa o construtor de uma classe é um método(método construtor, método especial), você sacou certo? Resumindo é um método cuja finalidade é construir… Obvio rsrsr!

Agora guarda essa dúvida aí, tem como destruir um objeto em Java? Se não sabe, vem comigo que vou te responder.

Como usar métodos construtores de classe em Java?

Primeiro você precisa saber que não vou aqui fazer tutorial de como criar um construtor de uma classe, isso é muito básico(até a própria IDE faz), o que vou fazer aqui é te trazer dicas básicas de onde é como usar, também algumas sacadas de cuidados na criação de um construtor de classe em Java.

Estamos acertados?

Como você fechou negócio comigo, vamos começar pelas boas práticas.

Boas práticas na criação de Construtores(Constructors) de classe em Java.

Validação de objetos.

Vamos lá, mentaliza aí uma classe Aluno, agora pensa um cenário onde você precisa validar os dados da criação de um objeto dessa classe na sua criação, é caso a validação de algum desses dados falhar(sei lá, o atributo nome não está passado na criação), então esse objeto não **deve ser criado, nesse caso um construtor ajuda bastante, pois logo na criação do objeto caso não atenda a definição de atributos determinada no método construtor vai ocorrer erro, (aposto que você pensou, aaa eu poderia validar com **if else, verdade, mas, sejamos elegantes sempre que possível rsrsr, agora vamos pensar em outro cenário, se você tivesse que validar também o objeto (MensalidadeAluno) somente se o objeto Aluno **tiver sido criado em um estado válido, **lembrando que esses objetos podem ser criados de forma independente, nesse caso também usando o construtor da classe podemos controlar isso. Por que, a validação do objeto Aluno irá acontecer sempre que um new Aluno for chamado em qualquer parte do código.

No processamento de um objeto.

Novamente mentaliza ai(mentalizou?) em quê? rsrsr, agora é sério, pensa aí em algum processamento de um objeto, pegando o ex: acima: Aluno, esse objeto para ser salvo na base de dados precisa estar pelo menos com sei lá três atributos inicializados, vamos pensar em (nome, telefone, tipo), usando construtores também poderíamos de forma legal validar isso para garantir que um novo objeto(Aluno) criado já esteja em um estado válido, conforme definido no método construtor, claro.

Mais uma… Ordem de execução.

Não sei se você conhece o pattern “Chain of Responsability”(Cadeia de responsabilidade), basicamente nesse pattern temos uma cadeia de objetos onde cada objeto fica responsável por validar uma regra de negócio. Fato é que em um exemplo de uso com 3 regras diferentes, então teríamos possivelmente 3 objetos para implementar esse pattern, eles devem ser chamados de forma encadeada, ou seja, primeiro chama o objeto 1 depois o segundo e assim por diante, até a regra ser validada. Agora imagina, nesse caso precisaremos que não haja falhas na ordem de execução, queremos que a ordem seja seguida certinha, 1..2..3.

Nesse cenário o uso de método construtor é muito útil, pois permite determinar a ordem que cada membro que compõe esse encadeamento seja inicializado, de forma automática ou manual.

Tá Gabriel entendi, mas… sem o uso de método construtor como ficaria?

Bom, sem nosso amigo construtor a JVM em tempo de execução(runtime) é quem vai determinar a ordem de inicialização de cada objeto, isso poderá seguir a ordem determinada ou não, isso mesmo, não necessariamente vai seguir o fluxo que você gostaria que os objetos fossem instanciados, enfim, não fica no controle do Dev sem uso de um construtor neste caso. Cá pra nós, lidar com situações que precisam de uma sequência e delegar isso para ser feito de forma automática… rummm é meio tenso rsrsr. Agora pegando esse entendimento poderíamos também validar corretamente o fluxo de um encadeamento ou algo assim, tipo usar uma validação para se certificar que objeto 2 foi chamado após objeto 1 é assim por diante.

O que não é legal fazer na criação de construtores de classe?

Muitos Construtores.

Esse artigo inclusive nasceu a partir do momento em que eu vi em um projeto qual eu estava atuando, algumas classes tinham construtores excessivos(na minha opinião) eram classes do tipo *DTO, tinham até 4 construtores diferentes, fui implementar um desses construtores e fiquei perdido, não sabia qual construtor usar, parei naquele momento reflexivo como melhorar isso? Foi aí que parei para pensar, isso é uma boa prática? Sim, me fiz essa pergunta porque um dos recursos da programação O.O é justamente a sobrecarga de método(override method), fiz algumas pesquisas, vi até um post no *GUJ com vários comentários sobre o assunto(tinha até comentários do Paulo Silveira(com foto de adolescente rsrs), CEO da Alura.

Achei interessante a discussão, o que pude abstrair foi que todos concordavam que não fica legal vários métodos construtores de formas diferentes, li até que algumas empresas definem que deve haver apenas 2 métodos construtores, 1 vazio, é 1 com as devidos atributos, justamente para não virar bagunça, então fica a dica, nada de chuva de construtores rsrsr.

Muitos parâmetros estão sendo passados no construtor.

Outra coisa importante, com base no nosso entendimento acima, podemos entender que não faz muito sentido ter um construtor se nenhum dos parâmetros vai receber dados obrigatórios para ter um estado válido, ou então se for possível que esses valores possam ser inicializados de forma padrão, pensa comigo, imagina uma quantidade monstra de parâmetros num construtor, a manutenção de um código com várias implementações assim, fica no mínimo triste, dá canseira só de olhar.

Afinal, devo ou não devo usar métodos construtores em Java?

Vamos alinhar um fato, construtores não são obrigatórios, toda classe precisa ter um construtor definido? A resposta é não, lembrando que estamos falando da linguagem Java, em outras linguagens não sei bem ainda como funciona, pode até haver alguma que obrigue a criação, mas em Java não.

Outra coisa, outro motivo que me levou a construir esse artigo é, percebi ser importante saber o porquê de criar um construtor é o por que não, em meus estudos vi uma declaração que dizia assim “Se você não sabe porque está criando um construtor provavelmente está criando-o desnecessariamente” por isso é importante entender como funciona o uso de construtor na criação de um objeto, sem esse entendimento não é possível entender quando o construtor é necessário. Agora olhando por outro ângulo, se nem mesmo passou por nossa mente que determinado objeto precisa da implementação de um construtor, que talvez seria melhor com o uso dele, bom nesse caso precisamos estudar mais, mas calma, por isso estamos aqui é por isso escrevi esse artigo, então tá tudo certo.

Matando a cobra e mostrando o pau.

(eu sempre quis escrever isso** rsrsr)**

No geral a preferência, sempre que possível, é que os membros sejam ou inicializados por padrão, ou individualmente no código que vai consumi-lo.

tá, como seria essa inicialização padrão?

variavel = valor

ou

Aquele velho é bom…

CLASSE classe = new CLASSE();

classe.setNome();

isso é tão familiar… rsrsr

Um construtor com dados definidos deve existir se realmente ele for necessário e só deve receber os parâmetros mínimos para gerar um estado válido manipulando a menor quantidade possível de membros.

Finalizando nosso papo, não fique sem criar um construtor quando ele vai trazer ao código legibilidade ou mesmo vantagem, seja para manutenção futura para facilitar testes, etc.

Tenha em mente…

  • Construtores nunca devem trazer efeitos colaterais

  • Pensando em **SOLID**, um dos princípios é justamente a questão da responsabilidade única, então um construtor só deve construir objetos válidos.

Assim…. respondendo a pergunta que fiz acima.

Não tem como destruir um objeto em Java.

até a versão 11 que foi até onde tenho estudado, o **Garbage Collector** o cara que poderíamos invocar via código para fazer isso, não tem como chamarmos ele via código Java, por que esse bonitinho é executado quando a JVM quer, da forma que ela quer é pronto, quem manda é ela rsrsr, o que podemos fazer é ela, atribuir null para uma para o objeto em questão, e retirar todas as referências que tenha para ele, dessa forma ele será elegível para a coleta, mas…. não há como saber quando ele realmente será coletado…

Tem muito mais para se aprender sobre método construtor em java: sugiro uma lida na documentação -> https://docs.oracle.com/javase/tutorial/reflect/member/ctor.html

Fechou? Papo meio denso mas te espero na próxima.

Disclaimer: This is a Blog i made with the intention of reinforcing my knowledge in javascript. I just thought sharing my blogs might be helpful for someone since they are written in tagalog (mostly in informal style). Incase of Misinformation I won't be responsible for it and if you have the time you can comment on the blog to address the mistake. Paggamit

item.title: 在不使用插件的情况下从 WordPress 主页隐藏特定类别的帖子 | Hide Posts From Particular Categories From WordPress Homepage Without Using a Plugin
item.description:
发表于默认情况下，您的 WordPress 网站的主页会显示所有类别的帖子。

但是，可以将某些类别排除在您的主页上显示，但在 WordPress 中没有开箱即用的选项。

我想在我的网站上隐藏交易类别的帖子，因为许多联盟计划误解了将优惠券发布为“交易网站”的网络营销博客，这是他们不允许的。

为避免混淆，我决定从我的网站主页中隐藏交易类别中的所有帖子。

在本教程中，我将向您展示如何在不使用插件的情况下创建类别过滤器。

您需要编辑站点的functions.php文件并添加以下代码：

 function hide_category_home( $query ) { if ( $query->is_home ) { $query->set( 'cat', '-9' ); } return $query; } add_filter( 'pre_get_posts', 'hide_category_home' );

将9替换为您的类别 ID。 不要删除'-'

使用以下代码隐藏多个类别的帖子：

 function hide_category_home( $query ) { if ( $query->is_home ) { $query->set( 'cat', '-9, -69, -23' ); } return $query; } add_filter( 'pre_get_posts', 'hide_category_home' );

点击保存，大功告成！

一旦暂停，digitalkube 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，digitalkube 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，digitalkube 发布的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 digitalkube 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 digitalkube：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 digitalkube 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Ritesh Saini for DigitalKube

Posted on Aug 4

By default, the homepage of your WordPress site shows posts from all the categories.

However, it is possible to exclude certain categories from being displayed on your homepage, but there's no option to do that in WordPress out of the box.

I wanted to hide posts from the deals category on my site because a lot of affiliate programs misunderstand internet marketing blogs that post coupons as "deals sites", which they do not allow.

To avoid confusion, I decided to hide all the posts from the deals category from my site's home.

In this tutorial, I will show you how to create a category filter without using a plugin.

You need to edit your site's functions.php file and add the following code:

function hide_category_home( $query ) {
if ( $query->is_home ) {
    $query->set( 'cat', '-9' );
}
    return $query;
}
add_filter( 'pre_get_posts', 'hide_category_home' );

Replace 9 with your category's ID. Don't remove '-'

Use the below code to hide posts from multiple categories:

function hide_category_home( $query ) {
if ( $query->is_home ) {
    $query->set( 'cat', '-9, -69, -23' );
}
    return $query;
}
add_filter( 'pre_get_posts', 'hide_category_home' );

Click save and you're done!
